differences.title: 1. DATA MODELING
differences.leftDesc: Represents data as interconnected entities with properties.
differences.rightDesc: Stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents.

differences.title: 2. QUERY LANGUAGE
differences.leftDesc: Declarative language for graph traversal and pattern matching.
differences.rightDesc: Rich query language for document manipulation and retrieval.

differences.title: 3. RELATIONSHIPS
differences.leftDesc: Relationships are primary, optimized for traversal.
differences.rightDesc: Relationships are modeled via embedding or linking.

differences.title: 4. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for social networks, fraud detection, recommendations.
differences.rightDesc: Suitable for catalogs, user profiles, and general data storage.

differences.title: 5. SCHEMA
differences.leftDesc: Flexible schema, can evolve with application needs.
differences.rightDesc: Highly flexible, no predefined structure required.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.rightDesc: Efficient retrieval of entire documents or specific fields.

differences.title: 7. SCALABILITY
differences.rightDesc: Designed for horizontal scaling via sharding.
